items.q: Which type of nuclear radiation is most ionising?
items.correct: Alpha radiation — large, doubly charged particles cause the most ionisation per unit distance.
items.misconception: Gamma is most ionising because it can travel furthest and pass through almost anything.
items.random_wrong: Beta radiation is most ionising because it consists of fast electrons.
items.why: Students conflate penetrating power with ionising power — gamma penetrates most because it ionises least, the opposite of intuition.

items.q: What is the difference between nuclear fission and fusion?
items.correct: Fission splits a heavy nucleus into smaller ones; fusion joins light nuclei into a heavier one.
items.misconception: Fission and fusion are the same process, just at different scales.
items.random_wrong: Fission combines small atoms into bigger ones, and fusion splits them.
items.why: The words sound similar (both start 'fi-'), and students mix up which one combines and which one splits.

items.q: Why is nuclear fusion much harder to achieve on Earth than fission?
items.correct: Fusion requires extremely high temperatures and pressures to overcome the electrostatic repulsion between positive nuclei.
items.misconception: Fusion is harder because the materials are rarer than uranium.
items.random_wrong: Fusion is harder because you need to break atoms apart first.
items.why: Students don't appreciate the Coulomb barrier; they assume the challenge is technological rather than fundamental physics.

items.q: A source has an activity of 800 Bq and a half-life of 4 hours. What is the activity after 12 hours?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 100 Bq. “50 Bq” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which is the BEST radiation for a medical tracer injected into the body?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Short half-life gamma. “Long half-life alpha” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A radioactive source has an initial activity of 3200 Bq. After 30 minutes the activity has fallen to 200 Bq. What is the half-life in minutes?
items.why: Exact answer: 7.5. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
